name: Rolled Animal Cookies
recipeInstructions:
Preparation time: 45 minutes Chilling time: Several hours Baking time: 7 minutes
1. Cream butter until smooth. Gradually add sugar and beat well. Beat in the egg, whipping cream, baking powder, baking soda, salt, and vanilla. Mix in sifted flour until a firm dough forms. Chill the dough until it is firm enough to roll out (several hours).
2. Preheat oven to 400 degrees F (200 degrees C). Roll chilled dough on a floured surface to about 1/8-inch thick. Cut into desired shapes with a flour-dipped cookie cutter and place on an ungreased or lightly greased baking sheet.
3. Decorate cookies as desired using colored sugar, sprinkles, raisins, or chocolate chips for the eyes. Bake until lightly browned, about 5-7 minutes. Let cool on wire racks.
Note: The dough can be kept refrigerated for several days to a week if not rolled out immediately. Store in tightly covered tins; they keep well. In past holiday seasons, Beverly Bergstrom and her sister often inserted small pieces of drinking straw into the cookies before baking, creating little holes for hanging on their Christmas tree. They would carefully remove the straws just as the cookies finished baking, leaving a perfect hole to string through for display. Their grandmother pretended dismay at finding empty strings but eventually accepted the fun tradition.
